content.full_text:
October 29, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

SUBJECT: REQUEST FOR OPINION

Gentlemen:

I request your ruling on the following facts.

The son-in-law of a member of the City Council is a department head. The City operates under a modified City Administrator form of government, under which all department heads are appointed, disciplined and dismissed by the City Administrator. The City Council has no part in the process, but it does set salary levels for all City employees.

The City Administrator wishes to present to the City Council a proposed reorganization of departments, under which the department head in question will be assigned additional duties. For that reason, the recommendation will include an increase in salary for the department head.

The department head resides within the City, in a building owned by the same Council member. He pays a fair market rent.

My question is whether or not the Council member is required to refrain from participating in a decision which will increase the salary of the department head, when his son-in-law is the incumbent in that position, for either of the following reasons:

1. The relationship of father-in-law and son-in-law; or

2. The "source of income" question arising out of the landlord-tenant relationship.

Very truly yours,

William Camil
WILLIAM CAMIL
CITY ATTORNEY
